data.body:
PASS-1 review (T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety) [overlay]: PASS / not-applicable. No findings.

T11 fires only when a plan changes a schema / persisted format or backfills data. This plan does neither. The word "migrate" here is the code-substrate sense — re-expressing two LLM gate code paths (the bespoke plan-review orchestrator.py + the direct verify_completion call in completion.py) AS engine workflows. It moves execution logic onto the workflow engine; it does not alter any persisted event-log schema, on-disk format, or backfill existing rows.

Per-deliverable check against T11's binary criteria (all not-engaged):
- Migrate plan-review / completion-verifier onto the engine → re-expression of in-process orchestration as a declarative workflow; no DDL, no large-table lock, no expand-contract surface.
- Planned-trace parity validation → runs OFFLINE via the FakeRunner/FakeAgentRunner seam (no live calls) over a scenario corpus; asserts on intended execution events, touching no persisted data.
- "signed verdicts/attestations intact" (AC4) → PRESERVES the existing HMAC signing/attestation format; it does not change the signature schema or backfill old attestations.
- "fingerprint checkpoint" in the extracted sizing.py → an existing in-run resumability mechanism being relocated behind epic A's batch-runner seam, not a new persisted-format migration or data backfill.
- Remove the sample stubs (examples/code_review.yaml + templates.py scaffold) → deletes example/scaffold source files (with forwarding shims preserved for referenced public API); no persisted data is rewritten.

This is consistent with the related 5fd2 plan-review experiments, which established that rebar "migration" is the bash→Python strangler (not a data migration) and that the deterministic T11 keyword over-fired on exactly this class of plan (2/19, all false). Per the overlay's ANTI-FP rule (not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes), T11 PASSES.

affirmations:
- [T11] Not-applicable — no schema/persisted-format change and no data backfill; the migration is a code-execution-substrate move (orchestration onto the workflow engine), validated offline, with the existing signed-attestation format preserved rather than altered. None of T11's checks (expand-contract/blocking-DDL, batching/throttling, resumability, dual-write window, rollback/data-loss) engage.

No checklist items emitted for T11.
